💀 Dead generations — how a crashed run became a verdict

Fix for #3036. A claude -p call can exit 0 and still produce nothing gradeable. The harness graded it anyway, which silently flipped delta-gated verdicts in both directions. Real instance: Skill Eval run 29723212799.

Pick what died, and watch the verdict it fabricates

Scoring is delta-vs-baseline, so which side dies determines which lie you get.

Before this PR

After

The leak, exactly

One jq fallthrough did it. . means "the whole document" — so with no .result, the entire error envelope was stringified and handed to the grader as the model's prose.

-  jq -r '.result // .content // . | ... tostring'      ← falls through to the ENVELOPE
+  jq -r '(.result // .content // "") | ... tostring'    ← never the envelope

The grader's own stated reasons quoted these, verbatim, as if they were an answer:
   "duration_ms"  ·  "duration_api_ms"  ·  "num_turns"  ·  'max_turns'

Terminal vs transient — why classification is not binary

The first design retried everything. But a turn-exhausted run will exhaust its turns again, so retrying burns a second full-length call to hit the same wall. The adversarial verifier caught this.

shapeclassaction
error_max_turns / max_budgetTERMINAL (rc 3)abort now — a retry costs ~118s to fail identically
empty file / whitespace-onlyTRANSIENT (rc 1)retry once
is_error, no subtypeTRANSIENT (rc 1)retry once
success envelope, .result == ""TRANSIENT (rc 1)retry — a dead run wearing a live file
prose, or plain --print textALIVE (rc 0)grade it

Gradeability is checked after extraction, not on the raw file — otherwise an envelope with an empty .result passes a size check while carrying nothing.

Why this went unnoticed all day

The eval gate was red across many unrelated branches, including several of this session's own PRs — each time correctly dismissed as flaky, because a non-required check that fails intermittently looks exactly like flake.

It was not flake. It was a harness scoring garbage,
then reporting that score with full confidence.

14 tests now pin the behaviour: the two shapes that
produce it, and the three that must NOT be mistaken
for it.
